candidates.row_label: Amortization including in cost of revenue
candidates.why: Appears directly beneath the selected 'Cost of sales' row and functions as a component/detail of that selected bucket, not a separate same-level operating expense to add later.
Label suggests a COGS D&A row, but no values are shown in the excerpt; there is no evidence of a separate add-on amount beyond the selected 'Cost of sales' bucket.

variables.notes: Selected COGS parent bucket is 'Cost of sales'. The visible amortization line is presented as a component within that bucket, so adding it separately would double count.

candidates.why: Sits under the SG&A section and reconciles with 'Other Marketing expenses' to the selected 'Marketing expenses' total, so it appears embedded in the selected parent row rather than separate.
Appears as a component under the selected 'Administrative expenses' row, alongside other admin sub-lines, indicating it is already included in the selected bucket.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Appears as a component of the selected 'Research and development costs' row. Because that R&D row is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, this R&D amortization should not be added separately.
This looks like an SG&A D&A header, but no values are shown; the actual populated amortization rows underneath appear to be components already included in selected SG&A rows.
Relevant by label, but no values are shown; no separate add-on amount is evidenced.

candidates.row_label: Amortization including Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Relevant by label, but no values are shown; also would likely be a component of the selected R&D row if populated.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is the sum of 'Marketing expenses', 'Administrative expenses', and 'Research and development costs'. The visible D&A rows are presented as sub-components reconciling into those selected parent rows, so separate addition would risk double counting.
